scoring.scoreInterpretation: Brand is rarely cited in unbranded AI queries — foundational work required
scoring.method: Weighted mention presence across 19 organic prompts, two models

gapStatement: LinearB dominates AI recommendations for Engineering Intelligence Tool tools, scoring 58 points higher (58 vs 0/100). That visibility gap is the opportunity Found By AI is built to close.

results.query: best engineering intelligence platforms for software teams

responses.claude: Top engineering intelligence platforms for software teams include DX, LinearB, Hatica, and Codersrank, which provide insights into development velocity, code quality, and team performance metrics. These platforms help teams understand bottlenecks, track progress, and optimize workflows by analyzing data from version control systems and development tools.
responses.gpt: Some alternatives to LinearB for engineering intelligence and metrics include GitPrime, Pluralsight Flow, and CodeClimate. These tools provide insights into team productivity, code quality, and other important metrics.

results.query: top developer metrics and analytics tools

responses.claude: Leading developer metrics and analytics tools include Velocity, GitPrime (now Hatica), Code Climate, and New Relic, offering visibility into cycle time, deployment frequency, code review quality, and system performance. These tools help teams identify trends, measure DORA metrics, and make data-driven decisions about development processes.
responses.gpt: For engineering analytics and planning, alternatives to Jellyfish include Forecast, Jira, and Clubhouse. These platforms offer features for tracking progress, managing workloads, and enhancing collaboration within engineering teams.

results.query: engineering productivity measurement platforms for tech companies

responses.claude: Engineering productivity platforms like LinearB, Hatica, and Multitudes measure sustainable productivity by tracking cycle time, deployment frequency, and team capacity while accounting for developer wellbeing. These tools go beyond vanity metrics to provide actionable insights that help tech companies balance output with developer health and prevent burnout.
responses.gpt: Engineering productivity measurement platforms like Flow, Harvest, and Jira can help tech companies track their team's efficiency and output. They provide insights into task completion, time management, and workload distribution.

results.query: best developer wellbeing and burnout prevention tools

responses.claude: Top developer wellbeing tools include Yerbo, which focuses on preventing burnout through work pattern analysis and manager alerts, along with platforms like Multitudes and Hatica that integrate wellbeing metrics into productivity tracking. These solutions help identify overwork, excessive context-switching, and early burnout signals before they lead to resignations.
responses.gpt: Best developer wellbeing and burnout prevention tools include Humu, Peakon, and Officevibe. These tools focus on employee engagement, feedback collection, and mental health resources.

results.query: top engineering analytics software for team performance

responses.claude: Leading engineering analytics platforms for team performance include DX, LinearB, Hatica, and Code Climate, which measure DORA metrics, cycle time, and deployment frequency to show how engineering processes impact business outcomes. These tools provide dashboards and insights that help engineering leaders understand team velocity and identify optimization opportunities.
responses.gpt: Top engineering analytics software for team performance includes Tableau, Looker, and Datadog. These tools help visualize data and provide insights into team operations and effectiveness.

results.query: software to detect early warning signs of developer burnout before resignations

responses.claude: Platforms like Yerbo and Multitudes specifically detect early burnout warning signs by analyzing work patterns, after-hours commits, context switching, and review velocity, sending alerts to managers before key resignations occur. These tools combine behavioral data with surveys and pulse checks to identify at-risk developers proactively.
responses.gpt: Software options like 15Five, TINYpulse, and Officevibe can help detect early warning signs of developer burnout before resignations. These platforms focus on employee engagement and feedback.

results.query: platform that provides actionable engineering metrics not vanity metrics like lines of code

responses.claude: Platforms like LinearB, DX, and Hatica provide actionable engineering metrics focused on cycle time, deployment frequency, code review efficiency, and team capacity rather than vanity metrics like lines of code. These tools deliver insights that directly correlate with business value and help teams make meaningful process improvements.
responses.gpt: Platforms like LinearB and GitPrime provide actionable engineering metrics that focus on value creation rather than vanity metrics like lines of code. They help teams analyze workflow efficiency and collaboration.

results.query: tool to measure impact of AI coding tools on team productivity and wellbeing

responses.claude: Tools like Hatica, LinearB, and Velocity can measure the impact of AI coding tools on team productivity by tracking changes in cycle time, deployment frequency, and developer focus time before and after implementation. These platforms also monitor whether AI tools reduce toil or create new bottlenecks in the development process.
responses.gpt: Tools like BetterUp and Otta provide ways to measure the impact of AI coding tools on team productivity and wellbeing. They assess user experience and productivity outcomes following tool implementations.

results.query: software for tracking sustainable productivity with healthy developers

responses.claude: Platforms like Multitudes and Yerbo focus on measuring sustainable productivity by tracking healthy work patterns, preventing excessive context-switching, and ensuring developers maintain reasonable hours. These tools help engineering leaders optimize output while protecting developer wellbeing and long-term team health.
responses.gpt: Software solutions like Time Doctor, RescueTime, and Clockify help track sustainable productivity while promoting healthy work habits among developers. They monitor time spent on tasks and encourage breaks.

results.query: platform to get visibility into engineering workload and work patterns

responses.claude: Visibility tools like Yerbo, Multitudes, and Hatica provide detailed insights into engineering workload distribution, after-hours work, context-switching patterns, and time allocation across projects. These platforms help managers understand how developers spend their time and identify unsustainable work patterns that could lead to burnout.
responses.gpt: Platforms such as JIRA and Monday.com provide visibility into engineering workload and work patterns. These tools allow for tracking task assignments and project statuses in real-time.

results.query: alternatives to LinearB for engineering intelligence and metrics

responses.claude: Popular alternatives to LinearB include Sleuth, which focuses on DORA metrics and deployment frequency; Code Climate, offering code quality and velocity insights; GitPrime (now Multithreaded), providing engineering productivity analytics; and Atheneum, which tracks engineering metrics across tools. Other options include Hatica for team productivity, Allstacks for engineering insights, and custom dashboards built with tools like Grafana combined with Git analytics.

results.query: alternatives to Jellyfish for engineering analytics and planning

responses.claude: Alternatives to Jellyfish for engineering analytics include Sleuth, which emphasizes DORA metrics and deployment insights; Hatica, focusing on team productivity and engineering metrics; LinearB, offering comprehensive engineering intelligence; and Atheneum for engineering analytics. You might also consider Allstacks for engineering data insights, Fathom for delivery metrics, or building custom solutions using tools like Grafana, Mixpanel, or Tableau combined with your Git and project management data.

results.query: alternatives to DX for developer experience measurement

responses.claude: Alternatives to DX for developer experience measurement include Hatica, LinearB, Multitudes, and Yerbo, each offering different approaches to measuring engineering productivity and team health. These platforms provide overlapping but distinct metrics around cycle time, deployment frequency, code quality, and developer wellbeing.
responses.gpt: Alternatives to DX for developer experience measurement include tools like Productiv and GitHub's own insights feature. These can provide valuable feedback on developer engagement and satisfaction.

results.query: Uplevel vs LinearB for engineering metrics and delivery intelligence

responses.claude: Uplevel and LinearB both provide engineering metrics and delivery intelligence, but LinearB focuses more heavily on DORA metrics and workflow optimization, while Uplevel emphasizes team dynamics, collaboration patterns, and developer experience alongside delivery metrics. LinearB is often preferred for teams wanting deep DORA analysis, whereas Uplevel provides a broader view of engineering health including workplace culture and retention risk signals.
responses.gpt: Uplevel and LinearB both focus on engineering metrics and delivery intelligence, but they approach it differently. Uplevel emphasizes providing insights into team performance and collaboration dynamics, while LinearB concentrates more on optimizing workflows and delivery pipelines through its integration with developer tools.

results.query: Uplevel vs Jellyfish for portfolio planning and resource allocation

responses.claude: Uplevel focuses on team health and delivery metrics rather than portfolio planning, while Jellyfish is specifically designed for portfolio management, capacity planning, and resource allocation across multiple projects. If portfolio planning and resource allocation are your primary needs, Jellyfish is the more specialized choice; Uplevel is better for understanding team productivity and engagement.
responses.gpt: Uplevel and Jellyfish both aid in portfolio planning and resource allocation, but their features differ. Uplevel offers a focus on engineering team performance metrics, helping teams understand capacity and efficiency, whereas Jellyfish emphasizes strategic alignment and visibility across projects and teams for better resource management.

results.query: Uplevel vs Swarmia for engineering productivity and developer wellbeing

responses.claude: Uplevel and Swarmia both address developer wellbeing and productivity, with Swarmia specializing in flow state metrics and engineering culture, while Uplevel integrates wellbeing signals with broader team collaboration analytics. Both tools move beyond traditional metrics to include developer satisfaction, though they emphasize different aspects of the engineering experience.
responses.gpt: Uplevel and Swarmia both target engineering productivity and developer wellbeing but have unique strengths. Uplevel provides analytics to improve team performance and collaboration, while Swarmia emphasizes understanding developers' time and well-being to foster a sustainable work environment.

results.query: best engineering intelligence tool for developer burnout prevention

responses.claude: Platforms like Yerbo and Multitudes are specifically designed for burnout prevention and include features for detecting overwork, excessive context-switching, and unsustainable work patterns. Combined with traditional engineering metrics from tools like Hatica or LinearB, these create a comprehensive approach to engineering intelligence that prioritizes prevention.
responses.gpt: The best engineering intelligence tool for developer burnout prevention is likely Humu, which focuses on identifying and addressing workplace issues that contribute to burnout.

results.query: is Uplevel worth it for engineering teams

responses.claude: Uplevel is worth considering for engineering teams seeking to understand team dynamics, collaboration patterns, and developer wellbeing alongside delivery metrics, particularly those concerned with retention and manager visibility. Its value depends on whether your team prioritizes holistic team health insights over specialized DORA metrics or portfolio management, and whether you're willing to invest in behavioral analytics.
responses.gpt: Uplevel can be worth it for engineering teams, especially those looking to enhance visibility into performance metrics and collaboration. Its data-driven insights can help identify improvement areas, although its value largely depends on a team’s specific needs and existing tools.

results.query: best developer wellbeing platform for engineering managers

responses.claude: Leading developer wellbeing platforms for engineering managers include Yerbo, which provides burnout risk alerts and work pattern analysis, and Multitudes, which combines productivity metrics with team health insights. These tools give managers the data they need to support developer wellbeing while maintaining engineering performance visibility.
responses.gpt: For engineering managers, the best developer wellbeing platforms include LifeWorks and Headspace for Work, which provide resources for mental health and overall employee wellbeing.

results.query: top engineering analytics platform for actionable insights

responses.claude: Top engineering analytics platforms for actionable insights include LinearB, Hatica, and DX, which translate raw development data into strategic recommendations around cycle time improvement, deployment practices, and team capacity planning. These platforms focus on metrics that directly impact business outcomes rather than vanity metrics.
responses.gpt: Top engineering analytics platforms for actionable insights include Amplitude, Mixpanel, and Seq. These tools focus on tracking user behavior and performance metrics to inform decisions.

results.query: best tool to measure AI coding tool impact on engineering teams

responses.claude: Tools like Hatica, LinearB, and Velocity measure AI coding tool impact by comparing key metrics before and after adoption, including cycle time reduction, deployment frequency changes, and developer satisfaction. These platforms can attribute productivity changes to specific tool implementations and measure effects on both speed and quality.
responses.gpt: Tools like LinearB and GitHub's Metrics can measure the impact of AI coding tools on engineering teams' productivity by analyzing workflow improvements and output efficiency.

results.query: tools used by engineering managers for team performance insights

responses.claude: Engineering managers commonly use platforms like Hatica, LinearB, DX, and Yerbo for team performance insights, which provide dashboards on cycle time, deployment frequency, code quality, and work patterns. These tools help managers understand team health, identify bottlenecks, and support data-driven conversations with their teams.
responses.gpt: Engineering managers often use tools like Asana, Trello, and Monday.com for team performance insights. These tools help track progress and manage team dynamics effectively.

results.query: developer productivity tracking tools for engineering leaders

responses.claude: Developer productivity tracking tools for engineering leaders include Hatica, LinearB, Velocity, and Code Climate, which measure cycle time, deployment frequency, code review efficiency, and team capacity. These platforms help leaders understand sustainable productivity levels and make informed decisions about process improvements and resource allocation.
responses.gpt: Developer productivity tracking tools for engineering leaders include TimeCamp, Toggl, and Jira, which allow leaders to monitor team output and individual contributions.

results.query: work pattern analysis tools for software development teams

responses.claude: Work pattern analysis tools for software teams include Yerbo, Multitudes, and Hatica, which analyze after-hours commits, context-switching frequency, time allocation across projects, and focus time. These tools help identify unhealthy patterns that reduce productivity and increase burnout risk before they impact team performance.
responses.gpt: Work pattern analysis tools for software development teams include Time Doctor and Hubstaff, which track work habits and identify improvement areas in productivity.

results.query: team health monitoring tools for preventing developer burnout

responses.claude: Team health monitoring platforms like Yerbo and Multitudes specifically prevent burnout by tracking work distribution, after-hours activity, context-switching, and individual capacity levels. These tools send proactive alerts when unhealthy patterns emerge, allowing managers to intervene early and maintain sustainable team performance.
responses.gpt: Team health monitoring tools like TINYpulse, Officevibe, and 15Five help prevent developer burnout by providing feedback mechanisms and promoting team engagement.

results.query: what is Uplevel engineering intelligence platform

responses.claude: Uplevel is an engineering intelligence platform that combines delivery metrics, team collaboration analytics, and developer wellbeing data to provide insights into engineering team health and performance. It uses data from tools like GitHub, Jira, and Slack to identify risks like burnout and disengagement while tracking delivery metrics and team dynamics.
responses.gpt: Uplevel is an engineering intelligence platform that delivers insights into team performance, collaboration, and productivity. It aggregates data from various developer tools to help organizations make informed decisions about team dynamics and project efficiency.

results.query: Uplevel engineering analytics platform review and features

responses.claude: Uplevel's analytics platform features include DORA metrics tracking, collaboration pattern analysis, delivery forecasting, individual contributor insights, and wellbeing risk indicators like burnout signals. The platform emphasizes actionable intelligence for engineering managers, providing team health dashboards, peer benchmarking, and integration with common development tools, though some users note the learning curve for maximizing platform features.
responses.gpt: The Uplevel engineering analytics platform offers features such as real-time performance tracking, collaboration metrics, and team engagement insights. It focuses on delivering a holistic view of engineering practices, enabling teams to identify strengths and areas for improvement.

results.query: Uplevel vs traditional DORA metrics platforms for performance measurement

responses.claude: Uplevel extends beyond traditional DORA metrics platforms by incorporating team collaboration, developer wellbeing, and behavioral insights alongside delivery performance measurement. While traditional DORA platforms focus narrowly on throughput and stability metrics, Uplevel provides a more holistic view of engineering team health, though this broader approach may be less specialized than tools focused exclusively on DORA optimization.
responses.gpt: Uplevel differentiates itself from traditional DORA metrics platforms by providing a broader set of insights beyond just the standard performance metrics. While traditional DORA metrics focus primarily on deployment frequency, lead time, mean time to recover, and change failure rate, Uplevel offers deeper analytics related to collaboration, team dynamics, and developer experience.
